outbound_official.notes: The Portuguese Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MNE) publishes per-country travel advice for Portuguese citizens at portaldascomunidades.mne.gov.pt/pt/vai-viajar/conselhos-aos-viajantes, organised by continent (Africa 54, Americas 35, Asia 47, Europe 48, Oceania 15). Each country page states whether Portuguese ordinary-passport holders need a visa, can enter visa-free, require an ETA, or must obtain an e-visa. There is no single consolidated flat list - visa status must be read from each destination's page. The lists below reflect entries confirmed directly from official per-country pages. EU/Schengen member states allow free movement for Portuguese citizens as EU nationals and are visa-free by treaty. The UK now requires an ETA (£16, valid 2 years/multiple entries). The USA requires ESTA (Electronic System for Travel Authorization) - treated here as ETA category as it is an electronic pre-travel authorisation, not a traditional visa. Australia requires an ETA obtained via travel agents or airline offices. India requires a visa but an e-visa (online application before travel) is available.

visa_free.name: Schengen Area member states (26 countries: Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland)
EU member states not in Schengen (Bulgaria, Cyprus, Ireland, Romania) - free movement as EU citizens

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C) - Tourism/Family/Business
visa_types.purpose: Allows entry for tourism, family visits, business meetings, and other short-stay purposes in the Schengen Area for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
visa_types.notes: Standard Schengen short-stay visa fee is EUR 90 for adults; EUR 45 for children aged 6–12; free for children under 6. Reduced fee of EUR 35 applies to nationals of Albania, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Belarus, Georgia, North Macedonia, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia, and Ukraine under EU visa facilitation agreements. Reduced fee of EUR 67.75 applies to Cape Verde nationals. Visa does not guarantee entry - border control may still refuse. ETIAS authorisation expected to replace the visa-free regime for exempt nationals from Q4 2026.

visa_types.name: Airport Transit Visa (ATV) - Schengen
visa_types.purpose: Permits transit through the international zone of Portuguese airports without entering the Schengen Area, applicable to nationals of countries subject to ATV requirements.
visa_types.notes: Required for nationals of Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Democratic Republic of Congo, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, Iran, Iraq, Nigeria, Pakistan, and Somalia at all Schengen airports. Additionally required at Portuguese airports specifically for nationals of Guinea, Senegal, and Uzbekistan. Exemptions apply to holders of valid Schengen visas/permits, US/Canada/Japan visas or residence permits, and diplomatic passport holders.

visa_types.name: Schengen Seasonal Work Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to engage in subordinate seasonal employment in Portugal for a period of up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: This is a Schengen-category visa for short-term seasonal work (90 days or less). For seasonal work exceeding 90 days, a national Temporary Stay Visa for seasonal work is required instead.

visa_types.name: National Visa - Subordinate Work (Temporary Stay)
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to enter and remain in Portugal for subordinate (employed) work for stays of more than 90 days and up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Temporary Stay Visa valid for up to 1 year with multiple entries. Standard national visa fee EUR 110. Processing deadline 30 days. A separate Residency Visa for subordinate work (valid 4 months, 2 entries) is available for those seeking to obtain a full residence permit through AIMA.

visa_types.name: National Residency Visa - Subordinate Work
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to enter Portugal and apply for a residence permit for subordinate employment through AIMA.
visa_types.notes: Residency visa valid 4 months (approximately 120 days) with 2 entries. Holder must apply for a residence permit with AIMA (Agência para a Integração, Migrações e Asilo) within Portugal during validity. Processing deadline 60 days. Fee EUR 110.

visa_types.name: National Visa - Independent Work / Self-Employment (Temporary Stay)
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals to enter Portugal to carry out independent professional work or self-employed activity for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Covers freelancers and independent contractors. Also available as a Residency Visa (4 months, 2 entries) for applicants intending to obtain a full residence permit.

visa_types.name: National Visa - Highly Qualified Activity (Temporary Stay)
visa_types.purpose: Allows highly qualified third-country nationals to enter Portugal for scientific research, higher education, or highly qualified professional activity for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Fee waived for highly qualified researchers. Covers university professors, researchers, and senior professionals. Also available as Residency Visa (4 months, 2 entries). Tech Visa program (IAPMEI-certified companies) provides fast-track access to this category.

visa_types.name: D8 Digital Nomad / Remote Work Visa (Temporary Stay)
visa_types.purpose: Allows remote workers and digital nomads who earn income from sources outside Portugal to reside in Portugal and work remotely for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Established under Article 60(2)(c) of the Foreigners Law. Minimum income approximately EUR 3,480/month (4x national minimum wage as of 2025) for the preceding 3 months. Applies to both salaried employees working remotely and self-employed/freelancers. Also available as Residency Visa D8 (valid 4 months, 2 entries) for those seeking a 2-year residence permit from AIMA. Minimum stay for maintaining D8 residence permit: 183 days/year.

visa_types.name: D8 Digital Nomad / Remote Work Residency Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows remote workers to enter Portugal and apply for a 2-year residence permit through AIMA, establishing long-term residency as a digital nomad.
visa_types.notes: Residency visa valid 4 months with 2 entries. Holder must apply to AIMA for a 2-year residence permit within Portugal. Renewable; leads to permanent residency after 5 years. Processing deadline 60 days. Fee EUR 110.

visa_types.name: National Visa - Study (Temporary Stay)
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals to enter Portugal for secondary education, bachelor's, master's, PhD, or post-doctoral study programs for more than 3 months.
visa_types.notes: Fee waived for holders of Portuguese government study scholarships. Covers all levels of formal education. Also available as a Residency Visa (4 months, 2 entries) for students intending to apply for a student residence permit through AIMA.

visa_types.name: National Visa - Professional Training / Internship / Volunteer Work (Temporary Stay)
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to enter Portugal for professional training programs, formal internships, or volunteer work for more than 3 months.
visa_types.notes: Covers structured training programs, accredited internships, and recognised volunteer schemes. Also available as a Residency Visa (4 months, 2 entries).

visa_types.name: D7 Passive Income / Retirement Visa (Temporary Stay)
visa_types.purpose: Allows retirees and persons living from passive income (pensions, property income, dividends, or financial assets) to reside in Portugal for up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Applicant must demonstrate sufficient regular passive income. No statutory minimum income amount is specified on the official page - documentary evidence of income/assets required. Also available as a Residency Visa D7 (4 months, 2 entries) for those intending to obtain a long-term residence permit. Leads to permanent residency after 5 years.

visa_types.name: D7 Passive Income / Retirement Residency Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows retirees and passive income holders to enter Portugal and apply for a long-term residence permit through AIMA.
visa_types.notes: Residency visa valid 4 months with 2 entries. Holder must apply for a residence permit with AIMA within Portugal. Renewable; leads to permanent residency after 5 years of legal residence. Processing deadline 60 days. Fee EUR 110.

visa_types.name: ARI - Autorização de Residência para Investimento (Golden Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Grants residency to third-country nationals making qualifying investments in Portugal, including capital transfers, job creation, cultural heritage, or scientific research.
visa_types.notes: Regulated under Article 90-A of the Aliens Act (Law 23/2007), as amended by Law 56/2023 ('Mais Habitação', in force from 7 October 2023). Real estate purchase routes were eliminated by Law 56/2023. Current qualifying routes: (1) EUR 500,000 capital transfer into qualifying non-real estate investment funds; (2) EUR 500,000 to establish or reinforce a Portuguese company with job creation; (3) EUR 500,000 for scientific research; (4) EUR 250,000 for artistic production or national cultural heritage; (5) Creation of at least 10 job positions. Minimum physical presence: 7 days in year 1, 14 days per subsequent 2-year renewal. Path to permanent residency and citizenship after 5 years. Applications managed by AIMA.

visa_types.name: National Residency Visa - Family Reunification
visa_types.purpose: Allows family members of residents legally residing in Portugal to join them and apply for a residence permit through AIMA.
visa_types.notes: Fee waived for family reunification descendants of residency permit holders and family members exempt under EU legislation. Residency visa valid 4 months with 2 entries; holder must apply to AIMA for a residence permit within Portugal. Processing deadline 60 days.

visa_types.name: National Visa - Medical Treatment (Temporary Stay)
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to enter Portugal for medical treatment and permits accompanying family members of patients undergoing treatment.
visa_types.notes: Covers both the patient and accompanying family members. Fee waived for patients and companions under health cooperation agreements. Temporary Stay Visa valid up to 1 year with multiple entries. Processing deadline 30 days.

visa_types.name: National Visa - Amateur Sports (Temporary Stay)
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to enter Portugal for amateur sporting activities and competitions for stays of more than 90 days and up to one year.
visa_types.notes: Specifically for amateur athletes; professional sporting activities would fall under work visa categories. Temporary Stay Visa valid up to 1 year with multiple entries. Processing deadline 30 days. Fee EUR 110.

visa_types.name: National Visa - Religious Purposes (Temporary Stay)
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals to enter Portugal for religious activities or ministry for more than 3 months in exceptional cases.
visa_types.notes: Available in exceptional cases only. Temporary Stay Visa valid up to 1 year with multiple entries. Processing deadline 30 days. Fee EUR 110.

visa_types.name: Skilled Job Seeker Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows specialised technical professionals to enter Portugal to seek qualified employment, permitting highly qualified work until visa expiration or residence permit issuance.
visa_types.notes: Exists in Portuguese legislation under Article 57-A of the Foreigners Law but was not yet operational at consular posts as of the last official page update. Not currently accepting applications - awaiting regulatory implementation. Processing deadline would be 60 days once operational.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic / Service / Official Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to diplomatic personnel, official government representatives, and their dependants travelling to Portugal on official duty.
visa_types.notes: Holders of diplomatic passports are exempt from airport transit visa requirements under Article 3(5)(e) of Regulation (EC) No 810/2009. Bilateral diplomatic and service passport waiver agreements exist with numerous countries (Turkey confirmed: service and special passports exempt from Schengen short-stay requirement under agreement signed 14 July 2010). Diplomatic visas are issued outside standard procedures and fees are typically waived.

visa_free.nationality: EU Member State citizens (all 27 EU member states)
visa_free.notes: EU citizens enjoy freedom of movement under EU treaties; not subject to Schengen short-stay visa rules. No maximum stay restriction as long as registration requirements are met.

visa_free.nationality: EEA nationals (Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway) and Switzerland
visa_free.notes: EEA/Swiss nationals enjoy freedom of movement equivalent to EU citizens.

Holders of British National (Overseas) passports (BNO) are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement. Listed separately from UK nationals on the official Portuguese MFA page under 'British citizens who are not nationals of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland'.
Holders of British Overseas Territories Citizens (BOTC) passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement. Listed separately from UK nationals on the official Portuguese MFA page.
Holders of British Overseas Citizens (BOC) passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement. Listed separately from UK nationals on the official Portuguese MFA page.
Holders of British Protected Persons (BPP) passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement. Listed separately from UK nationals on the official Portuguese MFA page.
Holders of British Subjects (BS) passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement. Listed separately from UK nationals on the official Portuguese MFA page.

visa_policy.notes: Portugal is a Schengen Area member. Visa policy is governed by EU Regulation 2018/1806. EU/EEA/Swiss nationals enjoy freedom of movement and are not subject to the 90-day Schengen visa-free limit. Visa-free third-country nationals may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. Airport transit visa required for nationals of Afghanistan, Bangladesh, DRC, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, Iran, Iraq, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, and additionally Guinea, Senegal, and Uzbekistan at Portuguese airports. EES (Entry/Exit System) became fully operational across all Schengen countries on 10 April 2026 - all non-EU/EEA travellers have biometric data recorded at borders. ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) is expected to launch in Q4 2026 per the revised EU official timeline (fee: EUR 20) - when operational, visa-exempt third-country nationals will need to obtain an ETIAS authorisation before travel. No visa-on-arrival or standalone e-visa system currently exists for Portugal.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of Turkish Service (grey) and Special (green) passports are exempt from the Schengen visa requirement for entry to Portugal under the Agreement between the Portuguese Republic and the Republic of Türkiye on the Suppression of Visas for Holders of Service and Special Passports, signed on 14 July 2010, with effect from 30 March 2011. Exemption does not apply to ordinary Turkish passport holders, who remain subject to Schengen visa requirement. Note: Turkish special passports can no longer be used for national visa applications (only Schengen short-stay); ordinary passport required for national visas.

credential.subtype: uniform Schengen visa (Type C or Type D)

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid uniform Schengen visa (short-stay Type C) or long-stay national visa (Type D) or residence permit issued by any Schengen Member State are exempt from the airport transit visa (ATV) requirement when transiting through the international transit areas of Portuguese airports. This is an airport transit exemption only under Article 3(5)(a) of Regulation (EC) No 810/2009 (Schengen Visa Code) - it does not grant a separate right of short-stay entry beyond the existing visa/permit.

Third-country nationals holding a valid visa for Canada are exempt from the airport transit visa (ATV) requirement when transiting through the international transit areas of Portuguese airports, under Article 3(5)(c) of Regulation (EC) No 810/2009 (Schengen Visa Code). This is an airport transit exemption only and does not confer a right of general short-stay entry to Portugal.

Third-country nationals holding a valid visa for Japan are exempt from the airport transit visa (ATV) requirement when transiting through the international transit areas of Portuguese airports, under Article 3(5)(c) of Regulation (EC) No 810/2009 (Schengen Visa Code). This is an airport transit exemption only and does not confer a right of general short-stay entry to Portugal.

Third-country nationals holding a valid visa for the United States of America are exempt from the airport transit visa (ATV) requirement when transiting through the international transit areas of Portuguese airports, under Article 3(5)(c) of Regulation (EC) No 810/2009 (Schengen Visa Code). This is an airport transit exemption only and does not confer a right of general short-stay entry to Portugal.

credential.subtype: residence permit listed in Annex V of Schengen Visa Code (guaranteeing unconditional readmission)

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid residence permit listed in Annex V of the Schengen Visa Code issued by Canada, guaranteeing the holder's unconditional readmission, are exempt from the airport transit visa (ATV) requirement when transiting through the international transit areas of Portuguese airports, under Article 3(5)(b) of Regulation (EC) No 810/2009. This is an airport transit exemption only and does not confer a right of general short-stay entry to Portugal.

credential.subtype: residence permit listed in Annex V of Schengen Visa Code (guaranteeing unconditional readmission)

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid residence permit listed in Annex V of the Schengen Visa Code issued by Japan, guaranteeing the holder's unconditional readmission, are exempt from the airport transit visa (ATV) requirement when transiting through the international transit areas of Portuguese airports, under Article 3(5)(b) of Regulation (EC) No 810/2009. This is an airport transit exemption only and does not confer a right of general short-stay entry to Portugal.

credential.subtype: residence permit listed in Annex V of Schengen Visa Code (guaranteeing unconditional readmission)

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid residence permit listed in Annex V of the Schengen Visa Code issued by the United States of America, guaranteeing the holder's unconditional readmission, are exempt from the airport transit visa (ATV) requirement when transiting through the international transit areas of Portuguese airports, under Article 3(5)(b) of Regulation (EC) No 810/2009. This is an airport transit exemption only and does not confer a right of general short-stay entry to Portugal.

credential.subtype: residence permit listed in Annex V of Schengen Visa Code (guaranteeing unconditional readmission)

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid residence permit listed in Annex V of the Schengen Visa Code issued by the United Kingdom, guaranteeing the holder's unconditional readmission, are exempt from the airport transit visa (ATV) requirement when transiting through the international transit areas of Portuguese airports, under Article 3(5)(b) of Regulation (EC) No 810/2009 as amended post-Brexit. This is an airport transit exemption only and does not confer a right of general short-stay entry to Portugal.

credential.subtype: residence permit listed in Annex V of Schengen Visa Code (guaranteeing unconditional readmission)

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid residence permit listed in Annex V of the Schengen Visa Code issued by Andorra, guaranteeing the holder's unconditional readmission, are exempt from the airport transit visa (ATV) requirement when transiting through the international transit areas of Portuguese airports, under Article 3(5)(b) of Regulation (EC) No 810/2009. This is an airport transit exemption only and does not confer a right of general short-stay entry to Portugal.

credential.subtype: residence permit listed in Annex V of Schengen Visa Code (guaranteeing unconditional readmission)

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding a valid residence permit listed in Annex V of the Schengen Visa Code issued by San Marino, guaranteeing the holder's unconditional readmission, are exempt from the airport transit visa (ATV) requirement when transiting through the international transit areas of Portuguese airports, under Article 3(5)(b) of Regulation (EC) No 810/2009. This is an airport transit exemption only and does not confer a right of general short-stay entry to Portugal.

Holders of diplomatic passports of any nationality are exempt from the airport transit visa (ATV) requirement when transiting through the international transit areas of Portuguese airports, under Article 3(5)(e) of Regulation (EC) No 810/2009 (Schengen Visa Code). This is an airport transit exemption. Note: this is distinct from bilateral diplomatic passport visa waiver agreements for short-stay entry, which are governed separately.

cbi.notes: Portugal does not have a citizenship-by-investment program. The Golden Visa (ARI) is a residency-by-investment program; citizenship can only be pursued through naturalisation after meeting standard residency and integration requirements under the Nationality Act (Law 37/81 and amendments). Dual citizenship is permitted under Portuguese law - there is no statutory requirement to renounce Portuguese nationality upon acquiring another, nor to renounce another nationality to naturalise as Portuguese.

programs.name: Autorização de Residência para Investimento (ARI) - Golden Visa
programs.notes: Regulated under Article 90-A of the Aliens Act (Law 23/2007), as amended by Law 56/2023 ('Mais Habitação', in force from 7 October 2023). The 2023 reform eliminated the real estate purchase routes (EUR 500,000 and EUR 350,000 renovation) and the plain capital transfer route (EUR 1,000,000/EUR 1,500,000) that were available before October 2023. Current qualifying investment routes: (1) Capital transfer of at least EUR 500,000 for acquisition of units in non-real estate collective investment undertakings established under Portuguese law, with minimum 5-year maturity and at least 60% invested in companies based in Portugal; (2) Capital transfer of at least EUR 500,000 to establish a commercial company headquartered in Portugal combined with creation of 5 permanent jobs, or to reinforce existing Portuguese company capital with maintenance of at least 10 jobs (minimum 5 permanent) for a minimum of 3 years; (3) Capital transfer of at least EUR 500,000 for scientific research activities in public or private institutions within the national S&T system; (4) Capital transfer of at least EUR 250,000 for investment in artistic production or support of national cultural heritage; (5) Creation of at least 10 job positions. Note: some official consular pages (vistos.mne.gov.pt/en/ari-residency-permit-for-investors and Embassy Ankara page) still display legacy routes including real estate - those routes are no longer available for new applications under Law 56/2023. Minimum physical presence: 7 days in the first year, 14 days in each subsequent 2-year renewal period. Holders and family members may apply for permanent residence under Article 80 after 5 years. Applications managed by AIMA (formerly SEF).

programs.name: D7 Passive Income / Retirement Residency Visa
programs.notes: For retirees and persons living from passive income (property, intellectual or financial assets). Applicant must demonstrate sufficient income. No statutory minimum amount specified on official page - documentary evidence of income/assets required. Leads to 2-year renewable residence permit; permanent residency after 5 years of legal residence.

programs.name: D8 Digital Nomad / Remote Work Visa
programs.eligibility: Remote workers (both salaried employees and self-employed/freelancers) earning income from sources outside Portugal. Minimum income: 4x the national minimum monthly guarantee remuneration for the preceding 3 months (approx. EUR 3,480/month as of 2025 based on minimum wage). Valid employment/service contract or proof of services required.
programs.processing_time: Not specified on official page; consular appointment required
programs.notes: Established under Article 60(2)(c) of the Foreigners Law (Law 102/2017). Two visa options: (1) Temporary Stay Visa (up to 1 year, for short-term remote work); (2) Residency Visa D8 (valid 4 months with 2 entries - holder must apply for 2-year residence permit with AIMA within Portugal). The residency permit is renewable and can lead to permanent residency (5 years) and citizenship. Minimum stay for maintaining D8 residence permit is 183 days/year.

programs.name: StartUP Visa
programs.eligibility: Entrepreneurs from non-Schengen countries wishing to establish an innovative tech-based startup in Portugal. Must enrol in a certified incubator (from the National Network of Incubators - RNI), sign an incubation contract, and receive IAPMEI certification. Project must have potential to achieve EUR 325,000 turnover or asset value within 5 years post-incubation. Applicant must hold minimum EUR 5,146.80 in bank account.
programs.processing_time: Not specified on official IAPMEI page; residency visa valid 4 months + 90-day extension for AIMA residence permit application
programs.notes: Managed by IAPMEI (Instituto de Apoio às Pequenas e Médias Empresas e à Inovação). Governed by Article 60(2)(c) of the Foreigners Act (Law 102/2017). Application submitted through IAPMEI online platform. Contact: startup.visa@iapmei.pt / +351 213 836 237.

programs.name: Tech Visa
programs.eligibility: Highly qualified and specialized workers recruited by IAPMEI-certified Portuguese companies or startups, who are non-Schengen area nationals. Workers must hold at minimum ISCED 2011 Level 6 (bachelor's degree) or Level 5 (higher vocational education) with 5 years of specialised technical experience. Proficiency in Portuguese, English, French, or Spanish required. Certified companies issue a digital Liability Note; workers present this at consular posts to qualify for a highly skilled worker residence visa/permit.
programs.processing_time: Simplified/fast-track process via company-issued digital Liability Note
programs.notes: Managed by IAPMEI. Companies must be certified under the Tech Visa program. Certified companies can recruit up to 50% of total workforce via Tech Visa (80% for companies in remote/peripheral areas). A mandatory minimum wage applies to Tech Visa workers. Program allows faster access to highly skilled worker residence visa or permit.

programs.name: Skilled Job Seeker Visa
programs.eligibility: Specialized technical professionals seeking employment in Portugal
programs.processing_time: Not yet operational - awaiting regulation of Article 57-A of the Foreigners Law
programs.notes: Visa category exists in Portuguese legislation (Article 57-A of the Foreigners Law) but was not yet available at consular offices as of the official page's last update. Not currently accepting applications.

validation_notes: CORRECTED (2026-06-14): ARI Golden Visa investment routes updated to reflect Law 56/2023 ('Mais Habitação', in force 7 October 2023). Prior record stated the real estate route was eliminated by 'Law 18/2022' - this is incorrect. The correct law eliminating real estate routes is Law 56/2023. The prior record's ARI notes were based on the Law 18/2022 reform which amended routes but did not yet eliminate real estate; Law 56/2023 is the correct eliminating instrument.

CORRECTED (2026-06-14): ARI notes updated to remove the EUR 500,000 real estate acquisition route and EUR 350,000 renovation route as qualifying options - these were eliminated by Law 56/2023 effective October 7, 2023.

CORRECTED (2026-06-14): ARI notes updated to clarify that some official Portuguese consular web pages (vistos.mne.gov.pt/en/ari-residency-permit-for-investors, Embassy Ankara, Toronto Consulate) still display legacy routes including real estate and a EUR 1M plain capital transfer - those pages have not been updated to reflect Law 56/2023 and should not be relied upon for current route eligibility.

CORRECTED (2026-06-14): ETIAS launch timeline updated in visa_policy notes from 'Q4 2026 (expected)' - this remains current per revised EU official timeline announced April 2025, confirmed as Q4 2026 following prior delays.
CONFIRMED (2026-06-14): Visa-free nationality list verified against vistos.mne.gov.pt live page - no additions or removals detected; list matches prior record.
CONFIRMED (2026-06-14): conditional_access entries verified - Turkish service/special passport exemption, Schengen Visa Code ATV exemptions (credential and diplomatic passport basis) remain current.
CONFIRMED (2026-06-14): cbi record (no CBI program) remains correct.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-14): Sri Lanka removed from airport transit visa list in notes - the official page fetch did not list Sri Lanka in the ATV-required list (confirmed absent from fetched content); original record already did not include Sri Lanka in the notes but it is listed in some prior versions. Kept as not in notes.
NOTE (2026-06-14): ARI min_amount field retains EUR 250,000 (lowest route: cultural heritage) as the minimum qualifying amount - this is correct for the current regime under Law 56/2023.

ADDED (2026-06-20): visa_types field populated with all official visa categories sourced from vistos.mne.gov.pt national visa and Schengen pages. Covers: Schengen short-stay (tourist/business/family), airport transit, Schengen seasonal work, national temporary stay visas (subordinate work, independent work, highly qualified, digital nomad D8, study, training/internship/volunteer, medical, sports, religious), national residency visas (subordinate work, independent work, family reunification, digital nomad D8, retirement D7), investment residency (ARI Golden Visa), and diplomatic visa. Skilled Job Seeker Visa noted as not yet operational.

ADDED (2026-06-27): Five British national subcategories confirmed visa-free on the official Portuguese MFA page (vistos.mne.gov.pt/en/short-stay-visas-schengen/general-information/who-needs-a-visa) under 'British citizens who are not nationals of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland': British nationals (Overseas) / BNO, British overseas territories citizens (BOTC), British overseas citizens (BOC), British protected persons (BPP), and British subjects (BS). These were missing from the prior record and have been added to visa_policy.visa_free.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): All previously listed visa-free nationalities re-verified against the official Portuguese MFA page. No nationalities added or removed from the standard visa-free list. Suriname was raised as a possible addition by an intermediate AI summary but the official page confirms Suriname is in the visa-REQUIRED list - NOT added.
UPDATED (2026-06-27): visa_policy notes updated to reflect EES (Entry/Exit System) full operational launch across all Schengen countries on 10 April 2026 per EU official sources.
UPDATED (2026-06-27): ETIAS fee noted as EUR 20 (updated from prior EUR 7 placeholder) per EU official sources; launch date remains Q4 2026.
CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): cbi record (no CBI program) remains correct.
CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): ARI Golden Visa routes and min_amount (EUR 250,000 for cultural heritage route) remain current - no changes to investment route structure detected from available sources.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): Full visa-free nationality list re-verified against live vistos.mne.gov.pt/en/short-stay-visas-schengen/general-information/who-needs-a-visa page (September 2025 page version). No nationalities added or removed. All listed visa-free nationalities confirmed present and no new Annex II additions/removals detected in 2025-2026 (other than the Kosovo transfer already recorded).

NOTED (2026-06-29): Georgia ordinary-passport visa-free status remains unaffected. EU suspended visa-free travel for Georgia holders of diplomatic, service, and official passports only (Regulation (EU) 2025/2441, effective 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027) — source: consilium.europa.eu/en/press/press-releases/2025/01/27/georgia-council-suspends-visa-free-travel-for-diplomats-and-officials/. This suspension applies to diplomatic/service/official passports only, not ordinary passport holders. No change required to visa_policy.visa_free Georgia entry.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): ETIAS fee EUR 20 confirmed from official EU source (home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/news/european-travel-authorisation-etias-will-cost-eur-20-2025-07-17_en). Launch remains Q4 2026; exact date not yet announced. EES confirmed fully operational since 10 April 2026 per home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/news/main-differences-between-ees-and-etias-what-travellers-need-know-2026-04-28_en.
CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): No changes to cbi (no CBI program), conditional_access, or rbi/fast_track entries required. All remain current.
gaps: AIMA website (aima.gov.pt) returned SSL certificate errors during re-validation, preventing direct extraction of current ARI investment amounts from the live official AIMA page. ARI investment routes cross-referenced from AIMA search snippet, EU legislative text via secondary sources, and Law 56/2023 analysis.

Some official Portuguese consular pages (vistos.mne.gov.pt/en/ari-residency-permit-for-investors, Embassy Ankara, Toronto Consulate) display outdated ARI routes including real estate as still active - those pages appear stale relative to Law 56/2023. The correct current routes are per the law text confirmed by multiple reliable cross-references.
Exact processing time for ARI/Golden Visa not confirmed on an official page.

Path to permanent residency for ARI officially stated as 'comply with Article 80 requirements' - the 5-year figure is consistent with the Aliens Act but not confirmed verbatim from a successfully scraped official page.
CPLP Agreement (Luanda Mobility Agreement, July 2021) - list of specific CPLP member states where the agreement has entered into force not enumerated on the official page.
D7 minimum income threshold not specified verbatim on the official page.
ETIAS exact launch date within Q4 2026 not yet announced per official EU sources.
Skilled Job Seeker Visa not yet operational pending regulation.

Comprehensive bilateral diplomatic/service passport waiver agreement list for Portugal could not be fully extracted - only the Turkey service/special passport agreement confirmed from official Portuguese source.
Sri Lanka is sometimes listed as requiring an ATV at Portuguese airports in secondary sources but was not confirmed present or absent in the fetched official page content.
Working holiday visa not listed - Portugal does not appear to have a bilateral working holiday agreement framework available on vistos.mne.gov.pt; this gap remains unconfirmed.

Schengen short-stay visa processing time not explicitly stated on official pages - standard Schengen processing is typically up to 15 calendar days per Schengen Visa Code Article 23 but not fetched verbatim.
fee_usd values not provided for any visa type - Portugal publishes fees in EUR only; USD conversion intentionally omitted to avoid stale exchange-rate errors.

EES impact on max_stay_days enforcement: EES became operational 10 April 2026 and tracks entries/exits of all non-EU nationals; the 90/180-day rule is now automatically enforced at Schengen borders. No change to the visa-free entitlement itself.

British national subcategories (BNO, BOTC, BOC, BPP, BS) added as visa-free based on the official Portuguese MFA page; their eligibility for ETIAS once launched has not been separately confirmed from an official source.

Comprehensive bilateral diplomatic/service passport waiver agreement list for Portugal remains only partially confirmed - only Turkey service/special passport agreement confirmed from an official Portuguese source.

Georgia diplomatic/service/official passport suspension (Reg. 2025/2441, 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027) is not reflected in conditional_access because this node records who Portugal admits (inbound to PRT), and the EU suspension affects all 30 Schengen/associated states. The conditional_access entry for 'diplomatic passports exempt from ATV' is a different provision (airport transit only). A conditional_access entry specifically recording that Georgian diplomatic/service/official passport holders now require a Schengen visa for entry was not added because the primary official Portuguese source (vistos.mne.gov.pt) had not yet been re-fetched to confirm how Portugal's own page reflects this suspension - it is noted here as a gap for the next validation cycle.
